compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) and P1 (String of Nickels) are present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are absent or indistinguishable from soil debris in the yellow pot.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains soil and debris; no visible plant matter. Likely systemic loss.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen remains stable with one primary leaf visible. Rabbit anchor is present.
plant_audit.P2_P4: No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest in the designated black pot; soil appears dormant.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Damp, dark, high organic content. Presence of crushed eggshell fragments across all pots.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant debris outside of pots.

temporal_deltas.T-3_to_T-2: Pothos leaf in P3 maintains posture; yellow pot remains empty.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Lighting shift (blue-spectrum interference) makes observation difficult; no structural change.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Return to baseline lighting; Pothos leaf remains turgid; no new growth.

anomalies: Widespread use of crushed eggshells as a soil amendment/top dressing.
Significant lack of foliage in P1 and P2/P4 pots compared to registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently dominated by a single Pothos specimen (P3). The other registered plants (P1, P2, P4) show no signs of life, suggesting either total senescence or failure to germinate/thrive. The environment is stable, but the botanical diversity is currently restricted to the Pothos. The rabbit anchor remains a consistent reference point for the P3 pot.
